DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF LAURALEE MARKUS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR MEDICAL RECORDS FROM CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER AND BAY AREA PAIN MEDICAL ASSOCIATES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO MODIFY IS GRANTED. THE SUBPOENAS ARE MODIFIED TO ONLY REQUIRE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS FROM JANUARY 1, 2000 TO THE PRESENT AND TO EXCLUDE ANY RECORDS RELATING TO OBSTETRIC AND GYNECOLOGICAL TREATMENT. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-08-2008 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 610

DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF LAURALEE MARKUS' MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS FOR PERSONNEL RECORDS FROM PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC, SAN FRANCISCO MAYOR'S OFFICE OF PROTOCOL, SAN FRANCISCO CHAMBER OF COMMERCE, AND STREETLINE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO MODIFY IS GRANTED. THE SUBPOENAS ARE MODIFIED TO ONLY REQUIRE PRODUCTION OF RECORDS RELATING TO APPLICATIONS FOR EMPLOYMENT, JOB DESCRIPTIONS, COMPENSATION, BENEFITS, OPPORTUNITIES FOR ADVANCEMENT, DISABILITY, ENGAGEMENT IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS, REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION, AND RELATED LEAVES OF ABSENCES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DYSLEXIA, AUDITORY PROCESSING ISSUES AND/OR THORACIC OUTLET SYNDROME AND/OR WORKERS COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD CASE NO. 1403. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.

DECLARATION OF GORDON J. FINE IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS' REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS TO DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT WOMEN'S INTITIATIVE FOR SELF EMPLOYMENT,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ABRAMS, JULIE VENEZIANO, DAVID

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WOMEN'S INTITIATIVE FOR SELF EMPLOYMENT, JULIE ABRAMS AND DAVID VENEZIANO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. WORKERS' COMPENSATION IS THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY AS IT RELATES TO THE INDIVIDUALS. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE D.F.E.H. RIGHT TO SUE LETTER. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES AND HAS EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE REASONABLY RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AS RELATES TO W.I.S.E. BECAUSE DISABLITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS ARE NOT A NORMAL PART OF THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Requesting Personnel Records From Former Employers Pacific Gas & Electric, San Francisco Mayor'S Office Of Protocol, San Francisco Chamber Of Commerce And Current Employer Streetline
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Lauralee Markus' motion to quash deposition subpoenas for personnel records from Pacific Gas and Electric, San Francisco Mayor's Office of Protocol, San Francisco Chamber of Commerce, and Streetline.
Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to quash is denied. The motion to modify is granted. The subpoenas are modified to only require production of records relating to applications for employment, job descriptions, compensation, benefits, opportunities for advancement, disability, engagement in the interactive process, reasonable accommodation, and related leaves of absences, including but not limited to dyslexia, auditory processing issues and/or thoracic outlet syndrome and/or Workers Compensation Appeals Board Case No. 1403. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Requesting Medical Records From Calif. Pacific Medical Center And Bay Area Pain Medical Asso.
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Lauralee Markus' motion to quash deposition subpoenas for medical records from California Pacific Medical Center and Bay Area Pain Medical Associates is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion to quash is denied. The motion to modify is granted. The subpoenas are modified to only require production of records from January 1, 2000 to the present and to exclude any records relating to obstetric and gynecological treatment. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WOMEN'S INTITIATIVE FOR SELF EMPLOYMENT, JULIE ABRAMS AND DAVID VENEZIANO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. WORKERS' COMPENSATION IS THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY AS IT RELATES TO THE INDIVIDUALS. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE D.F.E.H. RIGHT TO SUE LETTER. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES AND HAS EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE REASONABLY RELATED TO PLAINTIFF'S D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION AS RELATES TO W.I.S.E. BECAUSE DISABLITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS ARE NOT A NORMAL PART OF THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP. (302/PJM/X-MS) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
